prompt:
10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Software Engineering.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The method performs test-suite-based program repair by localizing suspicious statements, retrieving structurally compatible code fragments from other applications, adapting them via identifier and context mapping, and validating patched variants against the regression tests.
[2] Mech: It introduces a genetic-programming-based repair method that evolves populations of source-level program variants by deleting, inserting, or replacing suspicious statements with code fragments reused from elsewhere in the same program, scoring candidates by compilation success and test-suite performance.
[3] Mech: GenProg uses genetic programming to search over source-level program variants generated by mutating fault-localized statements with delete, insert, and replace edits that copy existing code fragments, scoring candidates by how many tests they pass.
[4] Mech: Random differential compiler testing automatically generates many small legal executable C programs, compiles them across different compilers or optimization settings, runs the binaries, and flags crashes or output inconsistencies as evidence of compiler bugs.
[5] Mech: CollAFL is a path-sensitive greybox fuzzing method that augments traditional edge coverage with compact branch-behavior-derived path identifiers so the fuzzer can distinguish and prioritize inputs that follow different decision contexts within already-covered code.
[6] Mech: AFL is a greybox fuzzing system that uses lightweight compile-time or binary instrumentation to record approximate edge coverage in a shared-memory bitmap, then retains inputs that trigger new coverage and mutates them with deterministic and randomized strategies in an evolutionary queue.
[7] Mech: DART combines automatic interface-level harness extraction with concolic execution: it runs the unit on random concrete inputs, records symbolic branch predicates along the executed path, negates one observed predicate to form a new path constraint, solves it, and re-executes with the resulting concrete inputs to drive exploration into alternative branches.
[8] Mech: SemFix repairs programs by fault-localizing suspicious statements, using symbolic execution on failing and passing paths to infer semantic repair constraints, and then synthesizing assignments or predicates at chosen modification points with constraint solving before test-suite validation.
[9] Mech: KLEE is a symbolic execution system that runs programs on symbolic inputs at the compiler IR level, accumulates path constraints, systematically negates branch conditions, solves the resulting formulas to generate new concrete tests, and models memory, libraries, and environment interactions to explore realistic systems code.
[10] Mech: It uses a learning-based repair method that mines historical bug-fix changes to train a probabilistic model which, given suspicious locations and contextual code features, ranks mutation-style candidate edits by how likely they are to match human-written fixes before validating them with tests.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Natural Language Processing.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The transformer architecture is an encoder–decoder architecture built entirely from stacked multi-head self-attention mechanism, encoder–decoder attention mechanism, and position-wise feed-forward layers, with positional encodings added to represent token order and masking used for autoregressive decoding.
[2] Mech: Model-B pre-trains a deep transformer architecture encoder with fully bidirectional self-attention mechanism using masked language modeling and next sentence prediction over inputs represented by summed token, segment, and position embeddings, then fine-tunes the same network with minimal task-specific heads.
[3] Mech: A large autoregressive transformer architecture decoder with causal masked self-attention mechanism is trained on massive web text using next-token prediction, and downstream tasks are performed by formatting inputs and desired outputs as a single text prompt for conditional generation without parameter updates.
[4] Mech: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) couples a dense neural retriever over a large text corpus with a conditional seq2seq generator and trains them end-to-end to generate outputs by conditioning on the input plus one or more retrieved passages, with variants that use fixed or token-level changing retrieval during decoding.
[5] Mech: The method combines supervised fine-tuning on human-written instruction demonstrations with reinforcement learning from a learned reward model trained on pairwise human preference rankings, using a KL penalty to keep the policy close to the supervised model during policy optimization.
[6] Mech: GPT-3-style autoregressive language model is a massively scaled decoder-only autoregressive transformer architecture trained on next-token prediction over a huge diverse text corpus and evaluated by placing natural-language instructions plus zero, one, or a few input-output examples directly in the context window without updating parameters.
[7] Mech: SQuAD constructs a benchmark by collecting crowd-authored questions on Wikipedia passages and defining each answer as a contiguous text span in the passage, enabling supervised training and evaluation as span prediction with exact match and token-overlap metrics.
[8] Mech: It introduces a neural reader that uses recurrent sequence encoders for both document and query and predicts an answer token or span by conditioning the document representation on the query in an end-to-end supervised model trained from raw text.
[9] Mech: Dense Passage Retrieval uses separate transformer architecture-based encoders for questions and passages to independently map them into a shared vector space, trained with supervised contrastive question–passage pairs and in-batch negatives, then retrieves passages by maximum inner-product nearest-neighbor search over precomputed passage embeddings.
[10] Mech: BiDAF uses contextual word-plus-character embeddings and recurrent encoders for passage and question, then builds a full passage-question similarity matrix to derive both context-to-query and query-to-context attention mechanism without early compression, fuses these token-level signals through modeling layers, and predicts answer start and end positions.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Robotics.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: A developmental self-exploration method lets the robot execute parameterized motor commands, observe sensory state changes, and incrementally learn predictive and competence-based sensorimotor mappings from actions and conditions to achievable effects and likely success.
[2] Mech: One-Shot Imitation Learning uses meta-imitation learning to train a task-conditioned policy that encodes one demonstration with temporal visual/state encoders and correspondence-style attention mechanism, then maps current observations plus the inferred task embedding to actions with optional one-step gradient-based adaptation.
[3] Mech: It uses deep reinforcement learning augmented with expert demonstrations by seeding an off-policy replay buffer with demonstration trajectories, applying reward shaping and reset/curriculum design during policy optimization, and using domain randomization to train sim-to-real robust dexterous control policies.
[4] Mech: PTAM uses a two-thread monocular SLAM architecture in which one thread tracks camera pose by projecting mapped features and minimizing reprojection error on each frame, while a parallel thread selects keyframes, triangulates new points, and runs local bundle adjustment to refine a sparse map.
[5] Mech: FAB-MAP represents each place as a bag of quantized visual words from local invariant features and performs Bayesian place inference with a generative model over binary word detections using a Chow-Liu tree to capture word co-occurrence dependencies and explicit false-positive/false-negative observation noise.
[6] Mech: Learning from Demonstration is formulated as recording demonstration trajectories, encoding task-relevant state and action variables, and fitting a policy that maps observed states to motor commands so the robot can autonomously reproduce the demonstrated behavior.
[7] Mech: MonoSLAM uses a single extended Kalman filter to recursively estimate a joint state over camera pose, motion, and sparse feature landmarks, with explicit motion dynamics, inverse-depth landmark initialization, and active uncertainty-guided feature search and update.
[8] Mech: A learning-from-demonstration method encodes a small set of demonstrations as segmented, parameterized manipulation trajectories over task-relevant state variables and contact events, then extracts spatial constraints, temporal ordering, and robot-centered policy parameters to adapt the skill online to novel situations.
[9] Mech: LSD-SLAM performs direct image alignment on high-gradient pixels for tracking, builds semi-dense inverse-depth keyframes via small-baseline stereo in a parallel mapping thread, and globally optimizes a similarity-transform pose graph between keyframes to correct monocular drift.
[10] Mech: DTAM alternates dense direct camera tracking by photometric image alignment against a reconstructed scene with variational per-pixel depth-map estimation for keyframes using multi-view photoconsistency cost volumes regularized by spatial smoothness, accelerated on the GPU.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in math.GR.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It derives similarity variables and an analytical local self-similar solution by reducing general strictly hyperbolic dynamics near shock onset to Burgers-like behavior.
[2] Mech: It defines local similarity by the pointwise limit d2(x,a)/d1(x,a) -> 1 as x -> a and proves equality of the induced intrinsic distances from this condition, while also deriving sufficient criteria and illustrating them on reproducing kernel Hilbert space examples.
[3] Mech: It defines a little mu-function as a degeneration of the generalized mu-function and derives it via q-Borel summation of a divergent solution to the Ramanujan difference equation, then develops symmetry, connection, contiguous, and Wronskian formulas for this new object.
[4] Mech: It proves structural results by analyzing spherical conjugacy classes and growth in abelian-by-cyclic semidirect products, then contrasts these with an explicit one-sided Følner sequence construction in BS(1,2).
[5] Mech: It derives sufficient geometric conditions by comparing corresponding sections or projections of the nested pair and by analyzing support cones or point-source shadow boundaries with apexes on the boundary of K.
[6] Mech: It introduces a new formal language of universes, induced communities, cells, and spots and uses this framework to derive a proof of the conjecture.
[7] Mech: It uses formal-language and group-theoretic arguments to prove non-EDT0Lness for the infinite cyclic group's word problem and to derive invariance under changing generators and passing to finitely generated subgroups.
[8] Mech: It defines this new operator class through measure-induced matrix entries and derives boundedness criteria by analyzing the interaction between the measure and the underlying weighted sequence norms.
[9] Mech: The paper develops inverse scattering for these nonzero step-like boundary conditions, formulates the solution as a Riemann-Hilbert problem, and applies nonlinear steepest descent to extract asymptotics in distinct space-time regions.
[10] Mech: It combines variational analysis via optimizers of an interpolation Hardy-Gagliardo-Nirenberg inequality, Pohozaev identity arguments, and a compactness method to derive ground states, nonexistence results, global/blow-up criteria, and minimal-mass blow-up characterization.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Robotics.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: A monolithic multigait soft robot uses elastomeric body segments with embedded fluidic channel networks that are selectively pressurized in different temporal patterns to produce distributed bending, anchoring, and release sequences for locomotion.
[2] Mech: Learning from Demonstration is formulated as recording demonstration trajectories, encoding task-relevant state and action variables, and fitting a policy that maps observed states to motor commands so the robot can autonomously reproduce the demonstrated behavior.
[3] Mech: Soft robotics paradigm: replace rigid-link, jointed, transmission-centric robot architectures with elastomeric or continuum bodies, distributed actuation, and morphology-driven control in which passive body mechanics and environmental contact perform part of sensing, adaptation, and control.
[4] Mech: BC-Z trains a single multitask behavior-cloning policy that maps visual observations plus a natural-language instruction or short task video into low-level actions using a shared cross-modal task embedding space to condition the policy.
[5] Mech: Series Elastic Actuation inserts a deliberately compliant spring in series between the motor/transmission and the load, measures spring deflection to infer output force via Hooke’s law, and closes the controller around force tracking rather than pure position tracking.
[6] Mech: An autonomous soft robotic fish uses onboard power, compact fluid sources, valves, and fluidic elastomer actuators embedded in a compliant body-tail structure to execute timed discrete pressurization sequences that drive large-amplitude bending for fast-start maneuvers.
[7] Mech: Relay Policy Learning trains a hierarchy of local controllers sequentially, where each new policy is learned via imitation-initialized reinforcement learning to steer states into regions judged solvable by a previously trained policy using its value or success criterion as the relay objective.
[8] Mech: The method learns a hierarchical latent-plan model from play data by inferring a discrete latent variable from an initial observation and a future goal state, then training a goal-conditioned low-level imitation policy to reproduce the intervening actions over variable horizons conditioned on the current state and inferred plan.
[9] Mech: One-Shot Imitation Learning uses meta-imitation learning to train a task-conditioned policy that encodes one demonstration with temporal visual/state encoders and correspondence-style attention mechanism, then maps current observations plus the inferred task embedding to actions with optional one-step gradient-based adaptation.
[10] Mech: An untethered soft pneumatic robot architecture integrates onboard power and pressure generation with embedded fluidic control and co-designed limb/body geometry so cyclic internal pressurization produces a coordinated crawling gait.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in stat.CO.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It proposes a simulation-based imputation procedure that combines Markov chain transition modeling with random-walk principles to fill in unobserved fish positions over time.
[2] Mech: The paper develops a composite-likelihood EM procedure that uses importance-sampling estimates, alongside a Monte Carlo EM baseline, to approximate maximum-likelihood inference without full high-dimensional integration.
[3] Mech: It uses a probabilistic analysis of evidentiary value to formally contrast profiling evidence with case-specific evidence and assess what each can rationally support.
[4] Mech: It develops a pairing theory in which electrons in the KTaO3 2DEG interact via exchange of the soft transverse optic Slater mode characteristic of a quantum paraelectric and evaluates the resulting superconducting coupling and gap anisotropy for different interface orientations.
[5] Mech: The paper implements mixture-of-experts distributional regression in an R package using TensorFlow-based stochastic first-order optimization with adaptive learning-rate schedules and robust estimation procedures across multiple response families.
[6] Mech: It uses atomistic simulations of a generic uncharged linear polymer in water with non-specific van der Waals polymer-water and polymer-ion interactions while varying NaSCN or NaI concentration at fixed Na2SO4 background to analyze ion accumulation, depletion, and polymer conformational changes.
[7] Mech: The paper uses optically excited gold-silica Janus nanoparticles whose asymmetric photothermal heating generates self-thermophoretic propulsion, quantified by single-particle tracking.
[8] Mech: The paper computes second-order reliability method estimates by finding the most probable path and numerically evaluating the associated infinite-dimensional fluctuation corrections, including operator traces and Carleman-Fredholm determinants, using scalable discretization-aware algorithms implemented with automatic differentiation in JAX.
[9] Mech: The paper proposes a hybrid sampler that alternates a Gibbs update for Pólya-Gamma latent variables with a NUTS step that uses Kalman filtering to marginalize latent states exactly.
[10] Mech: It uses a Bayesian latent class joint model for primary and key secondary endpoints with risk-factor adjustment to generate individualized dynamic k-step-ahead predictions and flag conflicting or anomalous future observations.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in physics.pop-ph.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The authors factorize the generalized Dirac oscillator into supersymmetric partner Schrödinger-like equations to obtain real spatial eigenvalues and then reconstruct relativistic energies through deformed Magueijo-Smolin and Amelino-Camelia DSR energy maps.
[2] Mech: It measures fragment anion yields for 1-propanol over 3.5 to 16 eV and uses DFT calculations to estimate threshold energies for the observed dissociation channels.
[3] Mech: The method uses a machine-learned predictor for the leftmost Hessian eigenvector to guide transition-state searches, with uncertainty-triggered fallback to a full Hessian update from the machine-learned potential when the prediction is unreliable.
[4] Mech: It proposes an analogical synthesis that maps emergence, geometric frustration, competing interactions, spin-glasses, and Mott insulators onto social dynamics and collective-individual relations within a universal consciousness framework.
[5] Mech: It reconstructs Fermi's path by analyzing his early interests in the absolute entropy constant, his engagement with shortcomings of Sommerfeld's quantization for identical particles, and his extension of Pauli's Exclusion Principle to non-interacting particle systems.
[6] Mech: The method separates neutron transport geometry from nuclear reaction processes and uses a neutron balance threshold condition with average path length, scattering distance, and leakage/absorption arguments to derive the critical radius without solving the diffusion equation.
[7] Mech: Using 9 fb−1 of LHCb pp collision data, the study reconstructs χc1(3872) candidates in the J/ψμ+μ− final state and measures the decay rate relative to the reference mode χc1(3872)→J/ψπ+π−.
[8] Mech: It uses a Schwinger-Keldysh full-counting-statistics formulation of Krylov position to derive an effective emergent phase-space action for operator growth in the presence of environmental coupling.
[9] Mech: It models consciousness-relevant physical implementations as effective causal graphs over a finite integration window τ and applies standard black hole causal structure to test whether a single strongly connected component can extend across the horizon.
[10] Mech: The authors combine molecular dynamics simulations of Lennard-Jones and covalently bonded nanoclusters with a continuum-theory treatment of oblique impact that incorporates elasticity, bulk viscosity, and surface tension.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Computer Vision.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It introduces an Efficient Sub-Pixel Convolutional Neural Network (ESPCN) that performs feature extraction entirely in low-resolution space and then uses a learned final convolution followed by a deterministic sub-pixel pixel-shuffle rearrangement to generate the high-resolution image.
[2] Mech: DCGAN uses a fully convolutional Model-GAN in which the generator maps latent vectors to images with stacked fractional-stride convolutions and the discriminator classifies images with strided convolutions, combined with architectural constraints such as removing pooling and most fully connected layers, extensive batch normalization, and asymmetric activations to stabilize training.
[3] Mech: pix2pix uses a conditional Model-GAN that generates a target image from a source image, trains a discriminator on input-output pairs to judge real versus synthesized outputs, combines adversarial loss with an L1 reconstruction loss, and uses a U-Net generator with a PatchGAN discriminator.
[4] Mech: Conditional Model-GAN augments the standard Model-GAN minimax game by feeding the same conditioning variable y into both the generator, which maps noise and y to samples, and the discriminator, which judges whether a sample-y pair is real or generated.
[5] Mech: LAPGAN uses a Laplacian pyramid of separately trained GANs, where a base generator samples a low-resolution image from noise and each higher conditional Model-GAN predicts the residual details needed to refine the upsampled image at that scale.
[6] Mech: EDSR uses a very deep residual convolutional architecture built from stacked residual blocks without batch normalization, performs most computation in low-resolution feature space, applies residual scaling to stabilize very deep/wide training, and upsamples near the output, with a multi-scale variant sharing one backbone across several scale factors.
[7] Mech: Generative Adversarial Nets train a generator that maps latent noise to synthetic data and a discriminator that predicts real versus generated inputs in a simultaneous differentiable minimax game, with the generator updated through gradients propagated across the discriminator.
[8] Mech: SRCNN uses a shallow fully convolutional network that takes an interpolated low-resolution image and jointly learns patch extraction, nonlinear low-to-high feature mapping, and image reconstruction end-to-end with supervised pixel-wise regression.
[9] Mech: VDSR uses a very deep stack of small-kernel convolutional layers on an interpolated low-resolution image and trains the network to predict the high-frequency residual to the ground-truth image, with high learning rates stabilized by gradient clipping.
[10] Mech: SRGAN uses a deep residual convolutional generator trained with a joint loss that combines adversarial supervision from a discriminator with perceptual content loss measured in the feature space of a pretrained recognition network rather than only pixel-wise error.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Natural Language Processing.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: LexRank builds a graph whose nodes are sentences and whose edges are tf-idf cosine similarities, then assigns sentence salience by recursively computing eigenvector-style centrality with thresholded or weighted links and damping-based score propagation.
[2] Mech: It uses a two-stage summarizer in which a reinforcement-learned sentence selector chooses salient source sentences and a neural rewriter locally abstracts, compresses, and paraphrases those selected sentences into the final summary.
[3] Mech: Constitutional AI trains an assistant with a written constitution by having the model self-critique and revise its answers under those rules for supervised finetuning, then generate constitution-based preference comparisons to train a preference model and optimize the assistant with reinforcement learning.
[4] Mech: It introduces an attention mechanism-based neural encoder-decoder that encodes the source sentence into distributed representations and uses a feed-forward conditional language model with soft alignment over source positions, plus optional source-word biasing, to generate the summary token by token.
[5] Mech: A large autoregressive transformer architecture decoder with causal masked self-attention mechanism is trained on massive web text using next-token prediction, and downstream tasks are performed by formatting inputs and desired outputs as a single text prompt for conditional generation without parameter updates.
[6] Mech: The transformer architecture is an encoder–decoder architecture built entirely from stacked multi-head self-attention mechanism, encoder–decoder attention mechanism, and position-wise feed-forward layers, with positional encodings added to represent token order and masking used for autoregressive decoding.
[7] Mech: Instruction tuning finetunes a large pretrained sequence-to-sequence model on a diverse mixture of heterogeneous NLP datasets reformatted into a unified text-to-text instruction/output format so the model learns to map natural-language instructions directly to task behavior.
[8] Mech: Pointer-generator networks augment an attention mechanism-based seq2seq summarizer with a learned timestep-wise gate that mixes fixed-vocabulary generation with copying from the source via the attention mechanism distribution, plus a coverage vector and penalty to discourage repeated attention mechanism.
[9] Mech: It extends attention mechanism-based sequence-to-sequence recurrent architecture summarization with stronger source alignment and copy-style decoding mechanisms, plus training and inference modifications that help preserve salient entities and phrases while generating summaries token by token.
[10] Mech: The method combines supervised fine-tuning on human-written instruction demonstrations with reinforcement learning from a learned reward model trained on pairwise human preference rankings, using a KL penalty to keep the policy close to the supervised model during policy optimization.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Robotics.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Third-Person Imitation Learning trains a policy with reinforcement learning to fool a behavior discriminator operating on transition features, while a representation network is adversarially optimized with a domain-confusion objective so those features predict expert-vs-novice behavior but not demonstrator-vs-learner domain identity.
[2] Mech: It learns a stable nonlinear dynamical system by fitting a Gaussian mixture model to joint state-velocity data and using Gaussian-mixture regression to blend locally affine linear dynamics whose parameters are constrained through optimization to guarantee global asymptotic convergence to the target equilibrium.
[3] Mech: An end-to-end deep visuomotor imitation policy combines a convolutional network over raw images with fully connected layers that fuse proprioceptive state and predict motor torques or control commands, with the visual front end often pretrained on auxiliary perception objectives before joint fine-tuning on demonstrations.
[4] Mech: One-Shot Imitation Learning uses meta-imitation learning to train a task-conditioned policy that encodes one demonstration with temporal visual/state encoders and correspondence-style attention mechanism, then maps current observations plus the inferred task embedding to actions with optional one-step gradient-based adaptation.
[5] Mech: A quadruped system is built around an actuator with switchable transmission that actively changes gear ratio between high-torque stance mode and low-impedance, high-speed swing mode, combined with proximal actuation, lightweight compliant legs, and morphology tuned to spring-mass running templates.
[6] Mech: Passive dynamic walking models a two-link biped descending a shallow slope as a hybrid dynamical system with continuous gravity-driven swing/stance motion and discrete heel-strike collision maps, then analyzes periodic step cycles and their stability from the mechanism's physical parameters alone.
[7] Mech: It introduces a hybrid feedback control framework that stabilizes periodic bipedal gaits by continuously regulating reduced-order gait variables and virtual joint constraints during swing and stance, while applying event-based corrections at foot impact and analyzing step-to-step stability with return maps.
[8] Mech: DAgger iteratively runs a mixture of the current policy and the expert, queries the expert on learner-visited states, aggregates all collected state-action pairs across rounds, and reduces policy learning to a no-regret online learning problem over the growing dataset.
[9] Mech: Hybrid zero-dynamics embedding: the method imposes feedback-enforced virtual constraints on an asymmetric hopper and constructs an impact-invariant reduced manifold so the closed-loop hybrid system evolves exactly as a Spring Loaded Inverted Pendulum template.
[10] Mech: A balance-centered feedback control method uses simplified whole-body dynamics to regulate center-of-mass motion, body orientation, and ground reaction/contact forces across hybrid support phases instead of tracking fully precomputed gait trajectories.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Diffusion Models and Generative AI.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: SDXL is a staged latent diffusion system that trains a larger high-resolution base model with a stronger text-conditioning pathway and revised denoising architecture in latent space, then applies a separate refiner model to add local detail and perceptual sharpness.
[2] Mech: Latent Diffusion Models generate images by training a diffusion noise-prediction network in the continuous latent space of a pretrained perceptual autoencoder, with attention mechanism and cross-attention mechanism used to model spatial structure and inject conditioning signals such as text or class labels.
[3] Mech: The challenge methods are centered on hybrid face restoration pipelines that use degradation-aware encoders to condition strong generative priors, with multi-scale fusion, facial-component emphasis, and perceptual/adversarial supervision to synthesize identity-consistent high-frequency facial details.
[4] Mech: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models define a fixed Gaussian forward Markov chain that gradually corrupts data and train a timestep-conditioned neural network to parameterize the reverse chain by predicting the added noise under a simplified variational objective.
[5] Mech: Diffusion probabilistic modeling defines a fixed forward Markov chain that gradually adds small Gaussian noise to data until it reaches a simple tractable distribution, then learns a parameterized reverse Markov chain with shared parameters by optimizing a variational log-likelihood bound to invert each noising step.
[6] Mech: Noise-conditional score-based generative modeling learns a neural network to estimate the score (gradient of log density) of Gaussian-perturbed data at multiple noise scales and generates samples by annealed Langevin dynamics that progressively denoise random initialization from coarse to fine scales.
[7] Mech: Minos is a profiling-and-classification framework that collects fine-grained runtime telemetry, hardware counters, and workload features across controlled variations in input size, parallelism, and node allocation to place applications into interpretable multidimensional behavior classes spanning compute, memory, communication, and energy characteristics.
[8] Mech: A progressive future-envisioning method models long-range visual change as a rollout of small temporally coherent latent transitions, where each step conditions on the current state, accumulated memory, and uncertainty-aware context before a conditional decoder synthesizes the next visual state.
[9] Mech: Generative Adversarial Nets train a generator that maps latent noise to synthetic data and a discriminator that predicts real versus generated inputs in a simultaneous differentiable minimax game, with the generator updated through gradients propagated across the discriminator.
[10] Mech: It introduces score-based generative modeling through stochastic differential equations, learning a time-conditioned score function over a continuous forward noising process and generating data by simulating the corresponding reverse-time SDE or its probability-flow ODE, with predictor-corrector refinement.

[1] Mech: Model-R uses residual blocks that learn a residual function F(x) and add it elementwise to an identity or projected shortcut x, with bottleneck blocks for very deep models and global average pooling before the classifier.
[2] Mech: A deep supervised convolutional neural network is trained end-to-end on GPUs using stacked convolution and overlapping pooling layers, ReLU activations, fully connected classifiers, dropout, data augmentation, and model parallelism.
[3] Mech: Diffusion probabilistic modeling defines a fixed forward Markov chain that gradually adds small Gaussian noise to data until it reaches a simple tractable distribution, then learns a parameterized reverse Markov chain with shared parameters by optimizing a variational log-likelihood bound to invert each noising step.
[4] Mech: Latent Diffusion Models generate images by training a diffusion noise-prediction network in the continuous latent space of a pretrained perceptual autoencoder, with attention mechanism and cross-attention mechanism used to model spatial structure and inject conditioning signals such as text or class labels.
[5] Mech: It introduces score-based generative modeling through stochastic differential equations, learning a time-conditioned score function over a continuous forward noising process and generating data by simulating the corresponding reverse-time SDE or its probability-flow ODE, with predictor-corrector refinement.
[6] Mech: The method uses Histograms of Oriented Gradients (HOG), computing dense local gradient orientation histograms over small cells, normalizing them within overlapping blocks, and classifying fixed-size sliding windows across position and scale with a linear margin-based classifier.
[7] Mech: The transformer architecture is an encoder–decoder architecture built entirely from stacked multi-head self-attention mechanism, encoder–decoder attention mechanism, and position-wise feed-forward layers, with positional encodings added to represent token order and masking used for autoregressive decoding.
[8] Mech: It uses a denoising diffusion probabilistic model in which a U-Net-like network with attention mechanism is trained across many timesteps to predict added Gaussian noise under a carefully designed variance schedule, then samples by iteratively reversing the fixed noising process from pure noise.
[9] Mech: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models define a fixed Gaussian forward Markov chain that gradually corrupts data and train a timestep-conditioned neural network to parameterize the reverse chain by predicting the added noise under a simplified variational objective.
[10] Mech: VGG uses a homogeneous deep convolutional architecture that stacks many small 3x3 convolutions, with occasional 1x1 convolutions and sparse pooling, followed by fully connected classification layers.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Database Systems.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The method uses a learned piecewise key-to-rank predictor augmented with update-aware metadata and local correction structures that track positional shifts, so prediction is separated from efficient exact rank recovery after updates.
[2] Mech: DILI is a distribution-driven learned index that constructs and adapts its model partitions according to observed key-density patterns, allocating more modeling capacity to locally complex regions so key-to-position predictions have smaller residual search ranges and can be updated under distribution drift without full retraining.
[3] Mech: The method constructs a congressional sample, a composite synopsis that combines a population-proportional sample with a group-coverage-oriented component and answers group-by aggregates by appropriately weighting sampled tuples.
[4] Mech: It introduces random sampling as a first-class relational operation, defining tuple-selection procedures with characterized inclusion probabilities under database storage constraints, including sequential access, limited memory, and both with- and without-replacement sampling, while analyzing interaction with relational operators.
[5] Mech: RadixSpline builds in a single sequential pass a bounded-error piecewise linear spline over sorted keys and a radix directory from key prefixes to spline segments, so a lookup predicts position with the spline and then performs a short bounded local search.
[6] Mech: It introduces learned index structures, especially a recursive model index that trains hierarchical models to approximate the key-to-position mapping given by the data CDF and then uses tracked error bounds with bounded local search to recover exact results.
[7] Mech: Quickr is a lazy approximate query processing system that performs progressive sampling during query execution and incrementally refines result estimates with error bounds until a budget, deadline, or confidence-based stopping condition is met.
[8] Mech: It proposes a one-pass streaming wavelet synopsis that incrementally updates and retains only the most significant hierarchical wavelet coefficients of the evolving frequency signal to support approximate reconstruction and aggregate estimation without materializing the full data.
[9] Mech: BlinkDB is a distributed approximate query processing system that precomputes a family of workload-aware multi-dimensional stratified samples and, at query time, uses an optimizer to choose the smallest sample predicted to meet a user-specified latency or accuracy bound while returning aggregates with confidence estimates.
[10] Mech: ALEX is a hierarchical learned index that uses lightweight regression models to route keys to leaf nodes, where sorted arrays with reserved gaps absorb inserts locally, while cost models trigger local expansion, retraining, or splitting based on access and update behavior.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in physics.plasm-ph.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The method launches the LP0,7 high-order mode into a step-index multimode fiber and uses programmable macrobends near the input to locally perturb that mode’s dispersion, thereby steering nonlinear dynamics and the output spectrum without substantial mode mixing.
[2] Mech: It characterizes AXUV-HS5 photodiode nonlinear response with pulsed-laser calibration and then estimates plasma density, size, temperature, and duration by combining charge-conserving soft x-ray measurements with a spherical emission model and the Bennett relation.
[3] Mech: The authors analyze the classical motion of an open string coupled to a constant electric field and map it via the double-copy prescription to a closed string propagating in the corresponding double-copied spacetime background, then compare the resulting dynamics and pathologies.
[4] Mech: It combines quantum Monte Carlo calculations with many-body ab initio interaction potentials that explicitly represent the proton coordination environment across cluster sizes.
[5] Mech: It analyzes the infinite-permeability limit of the Laplace boundary-value problem to show that the exterior solution is fixed entirely by the body's outer boundary geometry, regardless of interior details.
[6] Mech: The authors develop an analytical and numerical formalism that folds Doppler-broadened XFEL spectra into SAXS coherence calculations and apply it to benchmark single-step and periodic-grating geometries to estimate intensity reduction, Bragg-peak suppression, and peak broadening.
[7] Mech: It analyzes the generalized magnetic field \(\vec{\mathcal{B}} = \vec{B} + \nabla\times((c/\omega_{pe})^2\mu_0\vec{j})\) and proves in 3D that this field is Lie-dragged by an evolution velocity even when ordinary magnetic field lines reconnect.
[8] Mech: The paper develops a time-domain Method of Auxiliary Sources coupled with a first-order Standard Impedance Boundary Condition to model scattering through surface fields only, avoiding computation of internal fields.
[9] Mech: It constructs a symmetry-representation-based real-space Hamiltonian that treats the electric field as a vector in near and far fields, can deform between tight-binding and long-range photonic limits, and independently tunes symmetry-resolved mode energies at high-symmetry Brillouin-zone points.
[10] Mech: It synthesizes the historical development, observational motivations, conceptual implications, and competing theoretical frameworks surrounding dark matter and alternative gravitational laws.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Computer Vision.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: GoogLeNet/Inception uses repeated multi-branch modules that run 1×1, 3×3, 5×5, and pooling paths in parallel, applies 1×1 convolutions for dimensionality reduction before costly spatial filters, concatenates branch outputs, and trains with auxiliary classifiers plus global average pooling.
[2] Mech: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models define a fixed Gaussian forward Markov chain that gradually corrupts data and train a timestep-conditioned neural network to parameterize the reverse chain by predicting the added noise under a simplified variational objective.
[3] Mech: Progressive Growing of GANs trains the generator and discriminator by starting at very low image resolution and incrementally adding higher-resolution layers with smooth fade-in transitions, alongside signal-control tricks and a minibatch standard-deviation feature in the discriminator.
[4] Mech: A deep supervised convolutional neural network is trained end-to-end on GPUs using stacked convolution and overlapping pooling layers, ReLU activations, fully connected classifiers, dropout, data augmentation, and model parallelism.
[5] Mech: VGG uses a homogeneous deep convolutional architecture that stacks many small 3x3 convolutions, with occasional 1x1 convolutions and sparse pooling, followed by fully connected classification layers.
[6] Mech: It uses a denoising diffusion probabilistic model in which a U-Net-like network with attention mechanism is trained across many timesteps to predict added Gaussian noise under a carefully designed variance schedule, then samples by iteratively reversing the fixed noising process from pure noise.
[7] Mech: Data-I constructs a hierarchical image dataset by mapping object categories to the WordNet lexical taxonomy, automatically harvesting candidate web images via query expansion and search, and then using distributed human verification to filter and label them at scale.
[8] Mech: StyleGAN2 replaces progressive growing and heuristic resolution changes with a fixed-resolution style-based generator that uses explicitly low-pass-filtered upsampling/downsampling and revised normalization/noise injection to preserve signal alignment across scales.
[9] Mech: It uses an end-to-end trained multilayer convolutional neural network with local receptive fields, shared weights, alternating learned filtering and subsampling stages, and backpropagation to jointly optimize feature extraction and classification from raw pixels.
[10] Mech: StyleGAN replaces direct latent input to the generator with a learned mapping network that produces an intermediate latent code whose per-layer style parameters modulate each convolution via adaptive normalization, while independent noise is injected at each layer and style mixing is used during training.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in math.GN.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It develops constructive and structural arguments in metric geometry and topological group settings to show non-plasticity for countable dense subsets of normed spaces and to build plastic dense subspaces or subgroups inside broader separable spaces and groups.
[2] Mech: It introduces pyzentropy, an open-source Python package that computes total thermodynamic entropy by recursively decomposing contributions from high-probability configurations in a first-principles framework.
[3] Mech: It formulates fixed point theorems under the perturbed metric space framework for F-perturbed mappings, proves them analytically, tests necessity through a counterexample, and then uses the theorem to derive existence of a boundary value problem solution.
[4] Mech: It defines res-constructible extensions via definable closure from a tuple in the valuation ring whose residues are dcl-independent over the base residue field, and then analyzes intermediate models through residue-field and value-group structure.
[5] Mech: It proposes PLANCK, a physics-inspired deep reinforcement learning solver built on hypergraph neural networks that directly models arbitrary high-order interactions and exploits gauge symmetry during training and inference.
[6] Mech: It defines several aura-specific compactness and connectedness notions, constructs subspace and product aura topologies, and proves implication, preservation, and equivalence theorems under conditions such as transitivity and aura separation axioms.
[7] Mech: The paper builds a terahertz time-domain spectroscopy setup integrated with a Bluefors dilution refrigerator and a 7 T split-coil magnet, then measures terahertz waveforms from a GaAs single quantum well between 0 and 6 T at 145 mK.
[8] Mech: It constructs linear orders from either refining chains witnessing chainability or inverse-limit representations by arcs, each combined with a fixed non-principal ultrafilter on the natural numbers, and then analyzes their order-theoretic and descriptive-set-theoretic properties.
[9] Mech: The paper applies Bayesian optimization over five TFT pulse-operation parameters using normalized degree of separation as the objective, then validates the resulting settings on 4-bit and 6-bit temporal encoding and image-sequence reconstruction, with SHAP used for parameter-importance analysis.
[10] Mech: It factors a target map through the full evaluation table, enriches countable oracle sets as represented spaces to define a Weihrauch-SCI rank via reductions to iterated limit operators, and introduces intermediate SCI hierarchies by restricting base-level post-processing regularity and query policies.

They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The paper builds a tensor from daily precipitation, maximum temperature, and minimum temperature data and uses a spatio-temporal principal component analysis to initialize CP decomposition while explicitly incorporating spatial and temporal structure.
[2] Mech: The paper introduces two model-free Q-learning algorithms, Q-EarlySettled-LowCost and FedQ-EarlySettled-LowCost, designed to settle policies early while maintaining low switching or communication frequency and near-optimal regret.
[3] Mech: It introduces P-tensors as a general mathematical formalism for defining higher-order permutation-equivariant message passing over subgraphs and related structures within graph neural networks.
[4] Mech: The paper uses a Gaussian process surrogate and acquisition functions derived from the joint posterior of GP gradients to sequentially select new evaluations that improve Sobol' index and DGSM estimation.
[5] Mech: It proposes a top-down tree-ordered testing procedure that starts at the overall null, recursively tests grouped and then individual block hypotheses, stops exploring branches after non-rejection, and uses path-based diagnostics with adaptive alpha adjustment when needed for strong FWER control.
[6] Mech: The paper formulates streaming inference as a forward-KL projection in predictive-distribution space and derives a fully recursive deterministic beam-search algorithm that keeps the renormalized top-S posterior-weighted regime paths with closed-form predictive updates.
[7] Mech: It applies Singular Learning Theory and the local learning coefficient to characterize competing near-zero-loss basins and track training dynamics as a phase transition between them.
[8] Mech: It decomposes calibration variance with a frequentist component analysis over 292 million trades and validates the structure with a Bayesian hierarchical model.
[9] Mech: It uses GDP and Corruption Perception Index time series in a coupled vector autoregressive stochastic equation system, combined with Granger causality and graph-theoretic network construction, to estimate interdependencies across countries.
[10] Mech: The paper formulates a Laplacian-constrained GGM that jointly incorporates node signal data and text-derived metadata and solves the resulting optimization with a majorization-minimization algorithm using closed-form updates.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Software Engineering.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Continuous integration is a development practice in which developers merge small changes into a single shared mainline frequently, and every merge triggers an automated build and broad regression test suite with immediate repair of any broken build.
[2] Mech: It uses a syntax-driven neural decoder that generates code as a sequence of grammar-constrained actions over an abstract syntax tree, predicting nonterminal expansions, production rules, and terminals while conditioning on the encoded input and the partially built tree, with copying or specialized handling for open-vocabulary tokens.
[3] Mech: CodeBERT is an encoder-only transformer architecture jointly pre-trained on paired code-documentation data and unpaired code corpora using masked language modeling plus ELECTRA-style replaced token detection with a lightweight generator to learn shared code-text embeddings.
[4] Mech: It uses a large-scale repository-mining before/after observational design that identifies the point of continuous integration adoption in many projects and compares longitudinal changes in commit behavior, pull requests, issue handling, and testing activity around that event.
[5] Mech: Autoregressive code language model evaluation treats source code as a token sequence, trains large next-token predictors on code, and systematically probes them across scales, prompts, and task formats using both open-ended generation and constrained prediction benchmarks.
[6] Mech: An empirical repository-mining analysis operationalizes build maintenance effort by identifying and measuring version-history changes to build scripts, configuration files, dependency declarations, and related metadata, then comparing their evolution over time with general development activity.
[7] Mech: It uses large-scale mining of GitHub repository metadata, pull request histories, and discussion traces combined with statistical modeling that treats pull request acceptance and integration latency as separate dependent variables and relates them to technical, social, contributor-level, and project-level factors.
[8] Mech: An empirical qualitative factor analysis treats the CI configuration as a first-class artifact and derives adoption-relevant factors by comparing practitioner-facing evidence across project integration setups.
[9] Mech: It introduces a decision-tree-based probabilistic code model that factorizes the probability of each code element into a sequence of learned context-conditioned branching decisions over sparse lexical and syntactic features.
[10] Mech: A phrase-based statistical machine translation approach is trained on parallel code–pseudo-code pairs to learn token/phrase alignments, translation probabilities, and a target-side language model, then decodes pseudo-code by jointly optimizing faithfulness to source code and fluency.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Database Systems.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It introduces an R* distributed transaction management architecture in which a global transaction coordinator drives two-phase commit and distributed concurrency control across sites while local resource managers independently handle execution, locking, logging, and recovery for subordinate actions.
[2] Mech: It implements a production Paxos-based replication system that separates the core safety protocol from engineering subsystems such as persistent logging, leader failover, replica recovery and catch-up, membership handling, checksums, retransmission/timeout control, monitoring, and service interfaces around an ordered durable log.
[3] Mech: Paxos uses proposers, acceptors, and learners in a two-phase protocol with monotonically increasing proposal numbers, where prepare requests establish an ordering and accept requests are constrained to preserve any value that may already have been chosen.
[4] Mech: Paxos uses numbered proposals and a two-phase quorum protocol in which proposers first obtain promises from a majority of acceptors not to accept lower-numbered proposals, then submit a value constrained by the highest-numbered prior acceptance reported, with learners determining the chosen value from quorum intersections.
[5] Mech: A two-pass approximate dictionary matching method first applies learned or hand-crafted string transforms to generate normalized forms or index keys for aggressive candidate pruning, then performs a more discriminative edit-sensitive or weighted similarity check on the reduced candidate set.
[6] Mech: It uses a machine-learning-based integration framework that converts value similarity, schema correspondences, structural context, source behavior, and transformation history into features for statistical match prediction while maintaining provenance links over candidate matches and intermediate decisions.
[7] Mech: A declarative schema-mapping framework models data integration through source-to-target mappings, global schemas, and integrity constraints, and uses these logical specifications to define inference and query-answering semantics under alternative architectures such as mediated and warehouse-based integration.
[8] Mech: A semantic-values interoperability method attaches structured machine-interpretable semantic descriptors to exchanged data elements and uses semantic comparison plus value-level transformation rules to determine equivalence, comparability, or convertibility across local schemas without requiring a single global schema.
[9] Mech: TSIMMIS uses a mediator-wrapper architecture in which source-specific wrappers convert diverse source formats and access methods into a common self-describing object-like representation, and mediators then restructure, combine, and filter that representation to answer cross-source queries.
[10] Mech: EPaxos is a leaderless consensus protocol in which replicas independently propose commands with dependency sets capturing conflicting predecessors, commit non-conflicting commands via a fast path, and use a slower recovery path to resolve disagreements or failures by deriving execution order from a partial-order graph rather than a single global log.

8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in cond-mat.quant-gas.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It introduces a preconditioned iPEPS optimization scheme that uses an efficient approximation to the metric tensor based on its leading term to rescale gradients and accelerate convergence.
[2] Mech: It formulates a minimal mean-field model with pairing and quadrupolar channels, computes the free-energy density, and solves coupled self-consistent equations to map phase diagrams versus interaction strength and temperature.
[3] Mech: It probes the dynamics of non-local observables, especially magnetization fluctuations and a semilocal string observable, and compares Rydberg-array experiments with numerical simulations and a simplified classical stochastic model.
[4] Mech: It introduces a variational framework that decomposes Bloch bands into generalized Landau levels and applies it, together with first-principles-derived moire Hamiltonians, Hartree-Fock band renormalization, exact-diagonalization-style spectral diagnostics, entanglement spectra, and interpolation to conventional Landau levels.
[5] Mech: It develops a Lindblad-Keldysh spectroscopic response formalism that computes susceptibilities and optical response functions for non-interacting open systems governed by quadratic Lindbladians.
[6] Mech: It uses large-scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations to map the model’s phase diagram under tunable long-range tunneling and repulsive hole-hole interactions.
[7] Mech: The paper prepares the condensate in a dark state of a Lambda-coupled three-level system using optical fields and then numerically studies the resulting dark-bright solitons and lattices, including after switching the fields off.
[8] Mech: It solves the time-dependent Schrödinger equation in the vector-potential gauge by expanding the state in instantaneous eigenstates of the electric-field-dependent Bloch Hamiltonian and analyzing distinct early-, intermediate-, and long-time regimes.

[1] Mech: The authors develop an analytical and numerical formalism that folds Doppler-broadened XFEL spectra into SAXS coherence calculations and apply it to benchmark single-step and periodic-grating geometries to estimate intensity reduction, Bragg-peak suppression, and peak broadening.
[2] Mech: It analyzes the infinite-permeability limit of the Laplace boundary-value problem to show that the exterior solution is fixed entirely by the body's outer boundary geometry, regardless of interior details.
[3] Mech: It combines quantum Monte Carlo calculations with many-body ab initio interaction potentials that explicitly represent the proton coordination environment across cluster sizes.
[4] Mech: It synthesizes the historical development, observational motivations, conceptual implications, and competing theoretical frameworks surrounding dark matter and alternative gravitational laws.
[5] Mech: It constructs a symmetry-representation-based real-space Hamiltonian that treats the electric field as a vector in near and far fields, can deform between tight-binding and long-range photonic limits, and independently tunes symmetry-resolved mode energies at high-symmetry Brillouin-zone points.
[6] Mech: The authors analyze the classical motion of an open string coupled to a constant electric field and map it via the double-copy prescription to a closed string propagating in the corresponding double-copied spacetime background, then compare the resulting dynamics and pathologies.
[7] Mech: It analyzes the generalized magnetic field \(\vec{\mathcal{B}} = \vec{B} + \nabla\times((c/\omega_{pe})^2\mu_0\vec{j})\) and proves in 3D that this field is Lie-dragged by an evolution velocity even when ordinary magnetic field lines reconnect.
[8] Mech: It characterizes AXUV-HS5 photodiode nonlinear response with pulsed-laser calibration and then estimates plasma density, size, temperature, and duration by combining charge-conserving soft x-ray measurements with a spherical emission model and the Bennett relation.
[9] Mech: The method launches the LP0,7 high-order mode into a step-index multimode fiber and uses programmable macrobends near the input to locally perturb that mode’s dispersion, thereby steering nonlinear dynamics and the output spectrum without substantial mode mixing.
[10] Mech: The paper develops a time-domain Method of Auxiliary Sources coupled with a first-order Standard Impedance Boundary Condition to model scattering through surface fields only, avoiding computation of internal fields.

10 genomes from 2 different research lineages (5 each) in Natural Language Processing.
They are shuffled. Only the mechanism genome field is shown.
Identify which 5 belong to Group A and which 5 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The Indra Representation Hypothesis introduces a factorized multimodal latent space in which encoders decompose inputs into modality-agnostic semantic factors and modality-specific surface factors, trained with reconstruction, cross-modal agreement, and topology-preserving alignment regularization over latent units and their relations.
[2] Mech: Conditional Random Fields are globally normalized log-linear models that directly parameterize p(y|x) over entire label sequences using transition and state feature functions over labels and arbitrary overlapping input properties, with exact linear-chain inference via forward-backward and Viterbi dynamic programming.
[3] Mech: A family of decoder-only autoregressive transformers is pretrained at scale on expanded public text-and-code corpora and then split into base models and chat models produced by supervised instruction fine-tuning plus human-preference optimization and safety red-teaming.
[4] Mech: It uses a unified end-to-end neural network with shared learned word embeddings, multilayer nonlinear feature extraction over local windows or sentence-level contexts, and task-specific structured decoding when label dependencies matter, trained directly from raw or minimally processed text.
[5] Mech: It extends a BiLSTM-CRF sequence tagger with hard parameter sharing across related tagging tasks and a jointly optimized semi-supervised auxiliary objective on unlabeled sequences so that shared lower layers learn general token representations while task-specific output layers handle each label space.
[6] Mech: Dynamic Chain Optimization decomposes LLM adaptation into modular inference/specialization stages and uses a controller to estimate each stage's marginal utility and resource cost so it can selectively route, reorder, skip, and schedule lightweight frozen-backbone updates across heterogeneous on-device resources under latency, memory, energy, and privacy constraints.
[7] Mech: A neural end-to-end NER architecture composes each token from word embeddings plus character-level representations, contextualizes the sequence with a bidirectional recurrent encoder, and often decodes jointly with a sequence-level label model to enforce valid tag transitions.
[8] Mech: A progressive future-envisioning method models long-range visual change as a rollout of small temporally coherent latent transitions, where each step conditions on the current state, accumulated memory, and uncertainty-aware context before a conditional decoder synthesizes the next visual state.
[9] Mech: Model-B pre-trains a deep transformer architecture encoder with fully bidirectional self-attention mechanism using masked language modeling and next sentence prediction over inputs represented by summed token, segment, and position embeddings, then fine-tunes the same network with minimal task-specific heads.
[10] Mech: Constitutional AI trains an assistant with a written constitution by having the model self-critique and revise its answers under those rules for supervised finetuning, then generate constitution-based preference comparisons to train a preference model and optimize the assistant with reinforcement learning.
